International NGOs operating in Afghanistan include WFP (World Food Programme), FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization), and ICRC (International Committee of the Red Cross). Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under sanksi constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council sanksi regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: kandidat UE country with small open ekonomi ($27B PDB). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% PDB) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require lingkungan acquis alignment, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between oil producers and government revenue melemah — EU lingkungan standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession lingkungan alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Ketika Albania must meet EU lingkungan standards, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.
Terapkan EU-aligned lingkungan monitoring and emisi disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to emisi makes W visible.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan contamination at oil production sites to AKM (National Environment Agency)
Document health impacts on communities near oil fields — report to Institute of Public Health
Use Albania's pelapor protection law (Law 60/2016) for reporting lingkungan violations
Albpetrol: Kembangkan decommissioning and site remediation plan for aging oil fields
Investasi di energi terbarukan diversification — Albania has significant untapped solar and wind potential
Terapkan EITI enhanced reporting standards including lingkungan metrics
AKM: Tegakkan lingkungan remediation requirements at legacy oil production sites
AKBN: Require lingkungan bonds for all new exploration licenses
ERE (Energy Regulatory Entity): Accelerate energi terbarukan permitting
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Kuvendi (Albanian Parliament) untuk menuntut lingkungan remediation of legacy oil sites
Support accelerated EU accession negotiations contingent on lingkungan acquis compliance
Advocate for inclusion of lingkungan cleanup costs in Albania's EITI reporting
Dorong untuk Energy Community Treaty enforcement on energi terbarukan targets
i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for lingkungan monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts lingkungan impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's pelapor protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's milik negara oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and pertambangan revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent regulator energi, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant energi terbarukan expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and lingkungan legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and lingkungan acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked ekonomi ($26B PDB) with significant pertambangan sector (min=6.79% of PDB, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% PDB). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's pertambangan sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Ketika downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate lingkungan bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Ketika pertambangan companies must pre-fund lingkungan remediation, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction without cleanup melemah — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all pertambangan operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to lingkungan monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Open-pit pertambangan inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations at mine sites to the Environmental and Mining Inspection Body
Document water pollution downstream of tailings facilities — report to the Ministry of Environment
Use Armenia's pelapor protection provisions under the Anti-Corruption Strategy for reporting
Terapkan international best practice tailings management (Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management)
Terapkan EITI enhanced reporting including lingkungan impact metrics
Commission independent water quality assessments for all operations
Ministry of Environment: Perkuat lingkungan impact assessment requirements for new pertambangan licenses
EMIB: Increase inspection frequency and enforce penalties for water pollution violations
Ministry of Territorial Administration: Require community consent processes for new mine approvals
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly) untuk menuntut mandatory lingkungan bonds for all pertambangan operations
Support strengthening of EITI reporting requirements to include lingkungan impact data
Advocate for EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) enforcement on lingkungan standards
Dorong untuk international arbitration standards that include lingkungan remediation in pertambangan investment disputes
i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with lingkungan regulations at pertambangan operations, conducting inspections, and imposing sanksi. Ministry of Environment — oversees lingkungan policy, issues lingkungan impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the pertambangan sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest pertambangan company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of pertambangan revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees pertambangan policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the pertambangan sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and masyarakat sipil representatives overseeing extractive sector transparansi.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on lingkungan protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the ekonomi. Agriculture (22.1% PDB) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) melemah — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to energi terbarukan and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Ketika renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks.

Terapkan EITI-standard revenue transparansi extended to per-block lingkungan impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to emisi and lingkungan costs makes W computable per production unit.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations at oil facilities to MIREMPET (Ministry of Mineral Resources, Petroleum and Gas)
Document community health impacts near production facilities through local NGOs
Report financial irregularities in Sonangol operations — Angola's anti-corruption framework (Lei 3/20) provides limited protections
Sonangol: Kembangkan strategic diversification plan with binding energi terbarukan investment targets
International partners (TotalEnergies, BP): Terapkan Paris-aligned emisi reduction plans for Angolan operations
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all production sharing agreements
ANPG: Require lingkungan impact assessments with community consultation for all new production blocks
Ministry of Environment: Perkuat enforcement of lingkungan regulations in the petroleum sector
Banco Nacional de Angola: Integrate climate risk into financial supervision of oil-dependent lending
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Assembleia Nacional untuk menuntut publication of per-block lingkungan impact data for all oil operations
Support EITI enhanced reporting with lingkungan metrics for Angola
Advokasi melalui the African Union for continental energi terbarukan investment targets
Dorong untuk Paris Agreement compliance monitoring through UNFCCC mechanisms
i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for lingkungan policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced transparansi standards.

AR.s: G20 ekonomi ($638.4B PDB) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag PDB share but outsized lingkungan footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the kepentingan tumpang tindih — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to energi terbarukan. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Ketika renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well emisi disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to emisi makes W computable from production statistics.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report lingkungan violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial otoritas lingkungan (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: Kembangkan Paris-aligned transition plan with binding emisi reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
Investasi di CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well emisi data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: Perkuat federal lingkungan standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: Reformasi natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Diputado Nacional or Senador untuk menuntut a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well emisi reporting
Perkuat Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement lingkungan provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American energi terbarukan integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's otoritas lingkungan, responsible for lingkungan permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial lingkungan coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal pelapor protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% milik negara), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for lingkungan policy and perubahan iklim. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting emisi of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU ekonomi ($534.8B PDB) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of PDB). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% PDB) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Ketika per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Terapkan front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Ketika consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products melemah.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Reformasiulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
Reformasiulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data
BMSGPK: Terapkan mandatory front-of-pack health labelling for UPF products
AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories
Hauptverband der Sozialversicherungsträger: Release anonymized diet-related healthcare cost data
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Nationalrat or Bundesrat untuk menuntut mandatory health-impact labelling on ultra-processed foods
Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
Dorong untuk WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited masyarakat sipil space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between SOCAR and international partners (BP) melemah — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance energi terbarukan and non-oil economic development. Ketika SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks.
Terapkan per ladang emisi disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes emisi data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document emisi underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report lingkungan concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: Kembangkan binding energi terbarukan transition plan with interim emisi targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG emisi transparansi standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: Terapkan ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: Perkuat lingkungan standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include lingkungan impact data per production block
Tariff Council: Reformasi energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Advokasi melalui the Tehran Convention for Caspian Sea lingkungan protection standards
Support EITI enhanced reporting with lingkungan metrics
Dorong untuk European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on lingkungan standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's otoritas lingkungan, responsible for lingkungan monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees lingkungan impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive pelapor protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent transparansi mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs lingkungan standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and lingkungan issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict ekonomi ($29.6B PDB) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% PDB). kandidat UE country. Significant coal pertambangan and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Ketika Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The kepentingan tumpang tindih between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory emisi monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned lingkungan reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report lingkungan violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level lingkungan inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): Kembangkan coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
Investasi di energi terbarukan capacity to replace aging coal plants
Terapkan emisi monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): Reformasi tariffs to reflect true lingkungan costs of coal generation
Entity lingkungan agencies: Tegakkan air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce lingkungan compliance
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Parlamentarna skupština untuk menuntut coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
Dorong untuk Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate lingkungan agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — lingkungan inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive pelapor protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in pertambangan communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and lingkungan acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large ekonomi ($450.1B PDB) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of PDB). High agricultural employment (11.2% PDB). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Reformasi agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Ketika subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam monoculture between farmers and input suppliers melemah.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)
Agricultural input companies: Kembangkan integrated pest management products as alternatives to chemical-intensive packages
Investasi di climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
Terapkan water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: Reformasi subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: Terapkan mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): Tegakkan regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Jatiya Sangsad untuk menuntut reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national PDB accounting
Dorong untuk international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive pelapor protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Kembangkanment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Kembangkanment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's lingkungan regulatory agency, responsible for lingkungan clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters ekonomi ($671.4B PDB). Services-dominant (71% PDB). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% PDB). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Terapkan front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information melemah kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Reformasiulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report misleading health claims to SPF Économie (Federal Public Service Economy)
Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian pelapor protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Reformasiulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing
Extend voluntary Nutri-Score adoption to all product lines
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio
SPF Santé publique: Make Nutri-Score mandatory for all packaged food products
AFSCA/FAVV: Perkuat enforcement of marketing claims on UPF products
INAMI/RIZIV: Publish annual reports linking food consumption patterns to healthcare expenditure
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers untuk menuntut mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy
Advocate for WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
Dorong untuk inclusion of diet-related health costs in national accounting frameworks
i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian ekonomi ($23.1B PDB) with dominant gold pertambangan sector (min=15.45% PDB). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% PDB). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold pertambangan generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal pertambangan), and community displacement. Ketika lingkungan and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Mandate lingkungan bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial pertambangan licenses. Ketika pertambangan companies must pre-fund lingkungan remediation and community compensation, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction without accountability melemah.

Publish per tambang lingkungan impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to lingkungan data makes W visible.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Open-pit gold pertambangan inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near pertambangan operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal pertambangan activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): Terapkan Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
Terapkan EITI enhanced reporting including lingkungan impact metrics
Investasi di mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal pertambangan support programs
Ministry of Mines: Perkuat lingkungan conditions in pertambangan permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal pertambangan regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include lingkungan and community impact data
Advokasi melalui ECOWAS for regional pertambangan lingkungan standards
Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
Dorong untuk international pertambangan companies to adopt binding lingkungan standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national lingkungan assessment authority, responsible for lingkungan impact assessments of pertambangan projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees lingkungan policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and masyarakat sipil space is restricted.

Major pertambangan companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international pertambangan standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for pertambangan policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and pertambangan technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on pertambangan revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes lingkungan obligations for pertambangan companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable pertambangan governance.

BG.s: anggota UE ekonomi ($113.3B PDB) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% PDB). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Ketika Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory per pabrik emisi and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report emisi violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's pelapor protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Maritsa East complex operators: Kembangkan coal phase-out plans with worker transition programs
Investasi di energi terbarukan capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)
Terapkan per-unit emisi reporting aligned with EU taxonomy
KEVR (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission): Reformasi electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
Ministry of Energy: Kembangkan binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal
Ministry of Environment: Tegakkan Industrial Emissions Directive compliance at all coal plants
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Narodno Sabranie (National Assembly) untuk menuntut a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition funding
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
Dorong untuk Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional lingkungan agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national lingkungan monitoring agency collecting emisi and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the milik negara lignite pertambangan company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent regulator energi, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B PDB) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC ekonomi. Ketika financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction breaks.
Publish per ladang lingkungan and emisi data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle emisi visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document emisi or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include energi terbarukan components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase energi terbarukan allocation in investment portfolio
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require emisi disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: Perkuat lingkungan standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation
Advokasi melalui GCC Secretariat for regional carbon pricing mechanisms
Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
Dorong untuk enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national emisi reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's otoritas lingkungan, responsible for lingkungan policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees lingkungan impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the milik negara oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited pelapor protection; masyarakat sipil space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Kembangkanment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a monarki konstitusional with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African ekonomi ($21.5B PDB) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% PDB, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% PDB). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Ketika soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung from agricultural output data.
bp: Reformasi cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Ketika input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between cotton traders and input suppliers melemah.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making lingkungan and health costs visible makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce ketergantungan sistem — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)
AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton): Promote integrated pest management to reduce chemical dependency
Investasi di organic cotton certification and premium markets
Terapkan soil health monitoring across cotton supply chains
MAEP (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformasi cotton input subsidy programs to incentivize sustainable practices
ABE: Perkuat lingkungan impact monitoring in cotton-growing zones
INSAE (National Statistics Institute): Include soil health and agricultural lingkungan indicators in national surveys
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Député in the Assemblée Nationale untuk menuntut reform of cotton input subsidies toward sustainable agriculture
Advokasi melalui ECOWAS for regional agricultural sustainability standards
Dorong untuk Cotton-4 (Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali) coordination on sustainable cotton production
Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's lingkungan agency, responsible for lingkungan impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of masyarakat sipil organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts lingkungan monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B PDB) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the ekonomi. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between BSP and the government in continued extraction melemah — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Ketika non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction breaks.
Terapkan per ladang emisi disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations at BSP facilities through Shell's global ethics and compliance channels
Document emisi data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and lingkungan concerns
BSP/Shell: Extend Shell's global net-zero commitments to Brunei operations
BIA (Brunei Investment Agency): Diversify sovereign wealth into energi terbarukan and technology
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all Brunei operations
PetroleumBRUNEI: Require emisi monitoring and disclosure for all production licenses
Department of Environment: Perkuat lingkungan impact assessment requirements
AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam): Integrate climate risk into financial supervision
Advokasi melalui ASEAN for regional energi terbarukan integration and emisi reduction targets
Support UNFCCC mechanisms for small petrostate transition planning
Dorong untuk APEC energy working group initiatives on fossil fuel subsidy reform
Leverage Brunei's ASEAN chairmanship rotations to advance regional climate commitments
i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Kembangkanment, responsible for lingkungan policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no pelapor protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on perubahan iklim provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent ekonomi ($54.9B PDB) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and pertambangan (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Pertanian pada 8.8% PDB with soy expansion driving deforestasi. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Ketika water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Ketika indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction without consent melemah.

Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making lingkungan costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report lingkungan violations at pertambangan operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Report safety violations at pertambangan sites to the Ministry of Labor
YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos): Terapkan DLE technology to minimize water consumption
Terapkan community benefit sharing agreements with affected indigenous communities
Terapkan international sustainability standards (IRMA — Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance)
Ministry of Mining: Perkuat lingkungan requirements for lithium extraction permits
MMAyA (Ministry of Environment and Water): Tegakkan water extraction limits in Salar de Uyuni basin
AJAM: Require lingkungan bonds for all pertambangan concessions
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Diputado or Senador in the Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional untuk menuntut mandatory prior consultation with indigenous communities for all lithium projects
Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation rights in extractive industries
Advocate for international standards on sustainable lithium extraction through the Lithium Triangle coordination (Bolivia, Argentina, Chile)
Dorong untuk inclusion of water depletion costs in pertambangan revenue accounting
i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the pertambangan administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing pertambangan concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and lingkungan rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state pertambangan corporation overseeing Bolivia's pertambangan sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's pertambangan ministry, responsible for pertambangan policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing lingkungan policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian demokrasi. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island ekonomi ($15.8B PDB) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of PDB).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Ketika beneficial ownership information and tax transparansi data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Terapkan full beneficial ownership transparansi in line with FATF recommendations. Ketika the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through transparansi, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients melemah.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of The Bahamas
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas
Report regulatory violations to the Central Bank of The Bahamas
Terapkan enhanced due diligence procedures for all client relationships
Terapkan beneficial ownership transparansi standards ahead of regulatory mandates
Integrate climate risk assessment into financial products and client advisory services
Central Bank: Perkuat beneficial ownership disclosure requirements for all licensed financial institutions
Securities Commission: Tegakkan FATF recommendations on transparansi and anti-money laundering
Compliance Commission: Increase supervision of designated non-financial businesses and professions
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Member of Parliament in the House of Assembly untuk menuntut enhanced financial transparansi legislation
Support OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Inclusive Framework participation
Leverage the Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) to link climate finance to financial transparansi commitments
Advocate for Caribbean regional coordination on beneficial ownership transparansi
i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Layanan Keuangan Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style parlementer demokrasi. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($19.4B PDB) with diamond pertambangan historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% PDB). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium melemah — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Ketika non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle lingkungan data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all pertambangan operations. Making lingkungan costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Open-pit diamond pertambangan inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines
Debswana: Kembangkan post-diamond economic transition plan with energi terbarukan investment
De Beers: Extend lifecycle lingkungan reporting to all Botswana operations
Terapkan Kimberley Process Certification Scheme enhanced standards including lingkungan metrics
Ministry of Minerals and Energy: Require lingkungan bonds for all pertambangan licenses
BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service): Ensure mineral royalties reflect true lingkungan costs
Okavango Diamond Company: Lead on sustainability certification for Botswana diamonds
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Member of Parliament in the National Assembly untuk menuntut a comprehensive post-diamond economic diversification strategy
Support African Union Mining Vision implementation for sustainable diamond governance
Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include lingkungan and social standards
Dorong untuk SADC (Southern African Kembangkanment Community) coordination on pertambangan sustainability
i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for lingkungan policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for pertambangan safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees pertambangan policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the parlementer website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including pertambangan.

BY.s: State-controlled ekonomi ($76B PDB) with significant manufacturing (20.3% PDB). Potash pertambangan (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash pertambangan generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Ketika downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: International sanksi (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the kepentingan tumpang tindih by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Ketika sanctioned export revenues fall below lingkungan remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based lingkungan monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making lingkungan damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Potash pertambangan inherently generates salt waste. However, solution pertambangan and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document lingkungan violations through secure channels to international lingkungan organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand lingkungan certification as condition of purchase (post-sanksi)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian pertambangan
Require independent lingkungan audits as part of off-take agreements
International sanksi bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export sanksi until lingkungan and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue lingkungan performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international sanksi on Belaruskali until lingkungan and governance reforms are implemented
Advokasi melalui UNECE for transboundary lingkungan monitoring of potash pertambangan impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for lingkungan and governance reform
Dorong untuk international lingkungan damage assessment of Soligorsk pertambangan district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, masyarakat sipil, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective pelapor protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international lingkungan organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's milik negara potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK sanksi since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under otoriter governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU sanksi framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including lingkungan governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished ekonomi ($71B PDB). Mining dominates (28.81% of PDB) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of PDB.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Ketika supply chain traceability makes mine-level lingkungan and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W menjadi dapat dihitung from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Ketika artisanal miners must meet labor and lingkungan standards to access global markets, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam unregulated extraction melemah.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking per tambang lingkungan and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal pertambangan with proper safety and lingkungan management can reduce ketergantungan sistem compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report lingkungan or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Terapkan full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Terapkan IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Investasi di formalization programs for artisanal pertambangan communities
Ministry of Mines: Tegakkan 2018 Mining Code lingkungan provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Perkuat mineral certification to include lingkungan and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal pertambangan registration and oversight
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement untuk menuntut enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code lingkungan provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Advokasi melalui the African Union for continental responsible pertambangan standards
Dorong untuk OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale pertambangan. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping pertambangan sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC.
