Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees pertambangan policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for pertambangan sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi ($15.7B PDB) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between international operators and government revenue melemah — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Ketika non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Terapkan EITI-standard per-block revenue and emisi disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to lingkungan data makes W computable per production unit.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Terapkan Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Kembangkan economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Terapkan per-block emisi reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Perkuat lingkungan conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include lingkungan impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Advokasi melalui CEMAC for regional lingkungan standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with lingkungan metrics
Dorong untuk Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest ekonomi in francophone West Africa ($87.1B PDB). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of PDB with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require deforestasi-free supply chains — when per-farm deforestasi data is linked to cocoa transactions, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Ketika cocoa buyers must verify zero-deforestasi, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm lingkungan data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal deforestasi for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Terapkan full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Investasi di cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Terapkan living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Tegakkan traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to lingkungan compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Perkuat enforcement against deforestasi in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Député in the Assemblée nationale untuk menuntut enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Advokasi melalui the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding deforestasi targets
Dorong untuk living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end deforestasi in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and lingkungan regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to deforestasi after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($330.3B PDB) with massive pertambangan sector (16.23% of PDB). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium pertambangan depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Ketika per tambang water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung from pertambangan output data.
bp: Terapkan water pricing that reflects true scarcity in pertambangan regions. Ketika miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam water-intensive extraction methods melemah — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory per tambang water balance, tailings chemistry, and emisi disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects lingkungan data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report lingkungan violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's pelapor law (Ley 21.015) and lingkungan complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Terapkan Science Based Targets for emisi reduction across pertambangan operations
Terapkan ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase lingkungan monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at pertambangan operations
Sernageomin: Perkuat tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Terapkan the National Lithium Strategy with binding lingkungan standards
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional untuk menuntut mandatory water-neutral pertambangan operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on lingkungan access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for pertambangan supply chains
Dorong untuk international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's lingkungan enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with lingkungan permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts lingkungan impact assessments for pertambangan projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's milik negara copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper pertambangan companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and pertambangan service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees pertambangan policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African ekonomi ($53.3B PDB) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% PDB — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue melemah — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Ketika REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued extraction breaks.
Terapkan EITI-standard per-block lingkungan disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to lingkungan data makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Kembangkanment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Terapkan lingkungan monitoring and emisi reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Kembangkan post-oil diversification strategy
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Perkuat lingkungan conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Tegakkan lingkungan impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to lingkungan data
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Député in the Assemblée nationale untuk menuntut transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Advokasi melalui CEMAC for regional lingkungan standards
Dorong untuk EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees pertambangan and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American ekonomi ($418.8B PDB) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in energi terbarukan. Ketika energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory per ladang emisi disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emisi makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Terapkan TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Investasi di energi terbarukan in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Perkuat lingkungan licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Reformasi energy tariffs to accelerate energi terbarukan deployment
ANH: Terapkan per ladang emisi monitoring
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Perkuat Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral emisi reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance lingkungan governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's lingkungan licensing authority, responsible for lingkungan impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% milik negara), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the regulator energiy commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional lingkungan authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income ekonomi ($95.4B PDB) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% PDB). Leader in ecotourism and lingkungan policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Ketika per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Reformasi agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Ketika export tax benefits require lingkungan certification, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production melemah.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and lingkungan complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Terapkan pesticide reduction targets
Investasi di organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Terapkan watershed-level lingkungan monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformasi export incentives to require lingkungan certification
MINAE: Perkuat pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative lingkungan impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa untuk menuntut pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's lingkungan reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Dorong untuk green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts lingkungan impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for lingkungan services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global lingkungan leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on lingkungan access rights.

CU.s: Command ekonomi ($107.4B PDB, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% PDB, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Ketika organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that ketergantungan sistem on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document lingkungan degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Perkuat lingkungan monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Advokasi melalui CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Dorong untuk relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a negara satu partai. Independent masyarakat sipil, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees lingkungan policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island ekonomi ($37.6B PDB) with dominant services sector (76.5% PDB, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership transparansi requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership transparansi. Ketika financial secrecy is eliminated, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity melemah.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's anggota UEship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Terapkan enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Terapkan full beneficial ownership transparansi ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Perkuat beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Tegakkan enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce transparansi standards
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) untuk menuntut full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced transparansi requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Dorong untuk EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an anggota UE with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial ekonomi ($347B PDB) with strong manufacturing (19.9% PDB, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). anggota UE. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Jasa pada 60.2% PDB.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite pertambangan is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the kepentingan tumpang tindih between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Ketika plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks.
Mandatory per pabrik emisi and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report emisi violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in pertambangan communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech pelapor protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority milik negara): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and energi terbarukan investment
Sev.en Energy: Kembangkan mine closure and site remediation plans
Investasi di nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Reformasi electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Perkuat air quality enforcement in pertambangan regions
Czech Coal Commission: Terapkan recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament untuk menuntut a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Dorong untuk Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for lingkungan law, conducting inspections and imposing sanksi. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees lingkungan policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% milik negara), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite pertambangan and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent regulator energi. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic ekonomi ($424.5B PDB) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Jasa pada 63.5% PDB. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Reformasi agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Ketika the kepentingan tumpang tindih between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates ketergantungan sistem can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish pelapor law (Lov om beskyttelse af pelapore, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Investasi di plant-based protein alternatives
Terapkan nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Terapkan Scope 3 agricultural emisi reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Reformasi CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Terapkan the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% emisi reduction by 2030)
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Folketing untuk menuntut enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from deforestasi regions
Dorong untuk Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for lingkungan regulation including agricultural emisi and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — lingkungan policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% emisi reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest ekonomi ($124.3B PDB) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and pertambangan (2.04% PDB — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Jasa pada 59.8% PDB. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Ketika per tambang water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Mandate lingkungan bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Ketika pertambangan companies pre-fund cleanup, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction without lingkungan accountability melemah.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from pertambangan revenue.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Open-pit pertambangan disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations to the Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources
Document community health impacts — report to health authorities
Report through Barrick Gold's compliance channels
Barrick Gold (Pueblo Viejo): Terapkan GISTM tailings management standards
Terapkan full water quality disclosure for all operations
Investasi di community development programs per pertambangan code requirements
Ministry of Energy and Mines: Perkuat lingkungan conditions in pertambangan concessions
Tegakkan Mining Law 146-71 lingkungan provisions
Publish pertambangan sector lingkungan compliance data
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional untuk menuntut mandatory lingkungan bonds for pertambangan operations
Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic
Advokasi melalui SICA for regional pertambangan lingkungan standards
Dorong untuk pertambangan revenue transparansi
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the pertambangan directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees pertambangan policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs pertambangan concessions and lingkungan obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential demokrasi.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B PDB) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Pertanian pada 14% PDB (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Ketika production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Ketika solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam gas-fired power generation breaks.
Terapkan per ladang emisi disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes lingkungan damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations through Sonatrach internal channels
Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines
Report concerns to international partner compliance systems (Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor)
Sonatrach: Kembangkan solar energy division using Saharan land bank
Terapkan flaring reduction program
International partners (Eni, TotalEnergies): Apply global emisi standards to Algerian operations
ALNAFT: Perkuat lingkungan conditions in exploration and production contracts
CREG: Accelerate domestic energi terbarukan deployment (the PNER 2030 targets 22GW solar)
ARH: Tegakkan flaring reduction regulations
Advokasi melalui OPEC for managed production decline aligned with Paris Agreement
Support Mediterranean Solar Plan and Desertec-style initiatives for Saharan solar export
Dorong untuk EITI membership for Algeria
Leverage Algeria's gas supply role to Europe for energy transition dialogue
i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive pelapor protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent ekonomi ($124.7B PDB) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Pertanian pada 9.5% PDB (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the kepentingan tumpang tindih. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Terapkan the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Ketika democratic decisions override extraction interests, the kepentingan tumpang tindih breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block lingkungan impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's lingkungan data (Amazon deforestasi, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report lingkungan violations to MAATE (Ministry of Environment, Water and Ecological Transition)
Document community health impacts from oil operations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Use Ecuador's Constitutional protections for lingkungan defenders
PetroEcuador: Kembangkan transition plan to phase out operations in ecologically sensitive areas
Terapkan TCFD-aligned disclosure
Investasi di remediation of legacy contamination (Lago Agrio)
MAATE: Tegakkan Yasuní referendum result and strengthen lingkungan licensing
ARCERNNR: Reformasi energy regulation to accelerate renewable deployment
SHE (Secretaría de Hidrocarburos del Ecuador): Align licensing with rights of nature framework
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Asambleísta in the Asamblea Nacional untuk menuntut implementation of the Yasuní referendum and extension to other sensitive areas
Leverage Ecuador's rights of nature framework in international climate negotiations
Support the Amazon Cooperation Treaty Organization (ACTO) for regional lingkungan governance
Dorong untuk debt-for-nature swaps to finance the transition away from oil dependence
i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest lingkungan lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for lingkungan governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital ekonomi ($43.1B PDB) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% PDB). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ emisi.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Ketika carbon pricing fully applies, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Ketika subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory per pabrik lifecycle emisi and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed lingkungan monitoring makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.
a: Report lingkungan violations to Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board)
Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities
Use Estonian pelapor law (rikkumisest teavitaja kaitse seadus, 2022)
Eesti Energia/Enefit: Accelerate transition from oil shale to energi terbarukan
Investasi di Ida-Viru economic diversification
Terapkan binding emisi reduction targets
Konkurentsiamet: Reformasi electricity tariffs to reflect full lingkungan costs
Ministry of Environment: Perkuat oil shale processing lingkungan standards
Terapkan just transition plan for Ida-Viru county
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Riigikogu untuk menuntut a binding oil shale phase-out timeline
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ida-Viru region
Advocate for Baltic energy market integration to enable energi terbarukan scaling
Dorong untuk HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea pollution from oil shale waste
i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's lingkungan enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees lingkungan policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the milik negara energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.
Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's regulator energi. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant emisi reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab ekonomi by population ($389.1B PDB). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Pertanian pada 13.7% PDB. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for emisi transparansi. Per-field emisi disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Ketika solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam gas-for-domestic-power melemah.

Terapkan per ladang emisi disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle emisi visible per unit of production makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report lingkungan violations to EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency)
Document emisi data through international partner channels (Eni, BP)
Report through EGPC internal compliance mechanisms
Eni (Zohr operator): Terapkan emisi reduction targets for Egyptian operations
EGPC: Kembangkan energi terbarukan investment division
Terapkan TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
Ministry of Petroleum: Require per ladang emisi monitoring and disclosure
NREA: Accelerate energi terbarukan permitting (Egypt targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035)
EEAA: Perkuat lingkungan standards for gas processing
Leverage Egypt's COP27 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Advokasi melalui Arab League for regional emisi reduction coordination
Support Mediterranean energy transition dialogue through Union for the Mediterranean
Dorong untuk Suez Canal green shipping corridor initiatives
i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's lingkungan regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on masyarakat sipil and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for energi terbarukan development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B PDB). Agriculture dominates (34.8% PDB, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of deforestasi (fr=5.6% PDB equivalent in forest rents). Ketika per-farm lingkungan data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include lingkungan conditionality. Ketika agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam clearing forest for short-term cultivation melemah.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and degradasi tanah published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report illegal deforestasi to regional lingkungan protection authorities
Document land grabbing or forced displacement — report to EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission)
Report through agricultural development agent (DA) networks at kebele level
Ethiopian Commodity Exchange (ECX): Integrate lingkungan certification into coffee and sesame trading
Agricultural investment companies: Terapkan lingkungan impact monitoring
Support Green Legacy Initiative reforestation targets
Ministry of Agriculture: Reformasi the Productive Safety Net Program to include lingkungan conditionality
EFCCC: Perkuat forest protection enforcement
EIA (Ethiopian Investment Authority): Require lingkungan impact assessments for agricultural investment licenses
Support African Forest Landscape Restoration Initiative (AFR100) targets for Ethiopia
Advokasi melalui African Union (headquartered in Addis Ababa) for continental agricultural sustainability standards
Dorong untuk international climate finance for Ethiopia's agricultural transformation
Support Nile Basin Initiative cooperation on sustainable watershed management
i: Regional lingkungan protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves lingkungan management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Kembangkanment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's otoritas lingkungan. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlementer republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Kembangkanment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic ekonomi ($298.7B PDB) with strong forestry sector. Jasa pada 62.2% PDB, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% PDB). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). anggota UE with robust lingkungan regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Ketika per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Reformasi EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Ketika subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam plantation monoculture melemah.

Mandatory per-harvest lingkungan data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report forestry violations to ELY-keskus (Centre for Economic Kembangkanment, Transport and the Environment)
Document biodiversity loss in logged areas — report to SYKE (Finnish Environment Institute)
Use Finnish pelapor law (ilmoittajansuojelulaki, 2023)
UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group: Terapkan continuous-cover forestry targets
Terapkan per-harvest biodiversity monitoring
Investasi di high-value wood products to reduce harvest volume dependency
MMM (Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry): Reformasi the National Forest Strategy toward biodiversity targets
SYKE: Publish annual forest biodiversity monitoring data at municipality level
Perkuat EU Nature Restoration Law implementation in forestry sector
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Eduskunta untuk menuntut reform of forestry subsidies toward biodiversity and carbon storage
Support EU Nature Restoration Law implementation
Advocate for HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea eutrophication from forestry runoff
Dorong untuk inclusion of forestry carbon accounting in Finland's climate targets
i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for lingkungan monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national lingkungan research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African ekonomi ($20.9B PDB) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between operators and government revenue melemah. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam extraction.
Terapkan per ladang emisi disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making lingkungan costs visible makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report lingkungan violations to ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux)
Document oil spills or contamination — report through operator compliance channels
Laporkan ke EITI Gabon
TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala: Terapkan emisi reduction plans for Gabonese operations
GOC: Kembangkan forest carbon credit revenue streams
Terapkan biodiversity offset requirements for all operations
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Perkuat lingkungan licensing requirements
ANPN: Tegakkan protected area boundaries against oil exploration
EITI Gabon: Expand reporting to lingkungan metrics
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for Gabon's forest conservation
Advokasi melalui OPEC for managed production decline from depleting fields
Dorong untuk international carbon market standards that properly value Gabon's forest carbon
Support transition governance toward lingkungan transparansi
i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — lingkungan directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus ekonomi ($34.2B PDB) with pertambangan sector (1.28% PDB, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% PDB). kandidat UE country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese pertambangan (Chiatura) and copper pertambangan generate significant water pollution. EU accession lingkungan alignment will require improved monitoring. Ketika water quality data is linked to pertambangan output, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: EU accession lingkungan alignment. Ketika Georgia must meet EU lingkungan standards for pertambangan, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam low-cost extraction without lingkungan controls melemah.
Mandatory per tambang water quality and emisi disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations to the Environmental Information and Education Centre
Document water contamination — report to NEA (National Environmental Agency)
Use Georgia's pelapor protection mechanisms
Georgian Manganese (Euroasian Resources Group subsidiary): Terapkan lingkungan management systems
Terapkan international pertambangan standards (ICMM, IRMA)
Investasi di mine site rehabilitation
NAPR (National Agency of Public Registry): Perkuat pertambangan license lingkungan conditions
NEA: Increase lingkungan monitoring capacity
Ministry of Economy: Align pertambangan regulation with EU lingkungan acquis
Hubungi perwakilan Anda member of the Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) untuk menuntut EU-aligned lingkungan standards for pertambangan
Support EU accession conditionality on lingkungan governance
Advocate for Energy Community Treaty enforcement on lingkungan standards
Dorong untuk EITI membership for Georgia
i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for lingkungan monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees lingkungan and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's pertambangan sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for pertambangan licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for lingkungan alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an kandidat UE country (2023) and aspirant to full anggota UEship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with lingkungan provisions.

GH.s: West African ekonomi ($82.3B PDB) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold pertambangan (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold pertambangan (galamsey) is a major lingkungan crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal pertambangan) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Ketika per tambang water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W menjadi dapat dihitung. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal pertambangan with lingkungan standards and alternative livelihood programs. Ketika legal small-scale pertambangan provides comparable income with less lingkungan damage, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam illegal extraction melemah.
Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to pertambangan areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.

Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal pertambangan with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report illegal galamsey operations to the Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining (IMCIM)
Document water contamination — report to WRC (Water Resources Commission)
Report through the Minerals Commission complaint mechanisms
Gold Fields, Newmont, AngloGold Ashanti: Support formalization of artisanal pertambangan near concessions
Terapkan community development agreements per Ghana's pertambangan law
Terapkan mercury-free processing technology transfer programs
Minerals Commission: Accelerate small-scale pertambangan formalization with lingkungan standards
EPA (Environmental Protection Agency): Perkuat enforcement against water pollution from pertambangan
Forestry Commission: Protect forest reserves from galamsey encroachment
Hubungi perwakilan Anda Member of Parliament untuk menuntut emergency action on the galamsey water contamination crisis
Support EITI enhanced reporting with lingkungan metrics
Advokasi melalui ECOWAS for regional artisanal pertambangan regulation
Dorong untuk Minamata Convention enforcement on mercury use in gold pertambangan
i: IMCIM (Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining) — established to coordinate government response to the galamsey crisis. WRC (Water Resources Commission) — manages Ghana's water resources and monitors water quality. Minerals Commission — the regulatory body for the pertambangan sector, responsible for licensing and compliance monitoring.

Gold Fields — operates Tarkwa and Damang mines. Newmont — operates Ahafo and Akyem mines. AngloGold Ashanti — major gold pertambangan company with Ghanaian operations. Ghana is Africa's largest gold producer.

EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Ghana) — responsible for lingkungan permitting, monitoring, and enforcement. Forestry Commission — manages Ghana's forest reserves, many of which are threatened by illegal pertambangan. EITI Ghana — Ghana has been EITI compliant since 2010.

Parliament of Ghana — unicameral legislature with 275 members. Contact through parliament.gh. Ghana is a presidential demokrasi with peaceful transfers of power. Minamata Convention on Mercury — Ghana has ratified this treaty requiring phase-down of mercury use in artisanal gold pertambangan.

GN.s: Resource-rich West African ekonomi ($25B PDB) with massive bauxite reserves (world's largest — 7.4B tons). Pertanian pada 31% PDB. Military junta since 2021 coup. Guinea supplies ~25% of global bauxite (the ore used to produce aluminum).

sectors.w: Guinea's bauxite pertambangan displaces communities and contaminates water sources. As aluminum buyers face ESG pressure (EU CSRD, Apple/Tesla supply chain requirements), making per tambang lingkungan data visible breaks system independence.
bp: International supply chain due diligence (EU CSDDD) requiring lingkungan and human rights compliance for bauxite. Ketika buyers must verify mine-level conditions, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam low-cost, unregulated extraction melemah.
Per-mine lingkungan and community impact disclosure through EITI and supply chain traceability. Ketika downstream manufacturers can see the welfare cost per ton of bauxite, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
Dapat dipatahkan sebagian. Open-pit bauxite pertambangan inherently disturbs landscapes. However, progressive rehabilitation and dust suppression can reduce ketergantungan sistem.
a: Report lingkungan violations to international monitoring organizations (Human Rights Watch, Inclusive Kembangkanment International)
Document community displacement — report through international accountability mechanisms
Report through pertambangan company compliance channels (Rio Tinto, Alcoa, Emirates Global Aluminium)
CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée), SMB (Société Minière de Boké): Terapkan community resettlement standards per IFC Performance Standard 5
Terapkan ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) certification
Investasi di progressive rehabilitation of mined areas
Ministry of Mines: Tegakkan 2011 Mining Code lingkungan provisions
CPDM (Centre de Promotion et de Développement Minier): Perkuat lingkungan monitoring capacity
EITI Guinea: Expand reporting to lingkungan and community data
Advokasi melalui ECOWAS for return to democratic governance and strengthened pertambangan regulation
Support EU CSDDD implementation requiring due diligence for bauxite imports from Guinea
Dorong untuk ASI certification as condition for international bauxite purchases
Support international accountability mechanisms for pertambangan-affected communities
i: Note: Guinea is under military rule (CNRD — Comité National du Rassemblement et du Développement) since September 2021. Independent masyarakat sipil operates under severe constraints. Bureau Guinéen des Études et Évaluations Environnementales (BGEEE) — lingkungan assessment authority. International monitoring: Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Kembangkanment International have documented impacts of bauxite pertambangan on Guinean communities.

CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée) — JV of Alcoa, Rio Tinto, and Dadco, one of the world's largest bauxite producers. SMB (Société Minière de Boké) — Chinese-backed consortium (includes Winning International, Shandong Weiqiao). GAC (Guinea Alumina Corporation) — Emirates Global Aluminium subsidiary. ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) — the industry sustainability standard for the aluminum value chain.

Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie — oversees pertambangan policy and licensing. Code Minier (2011, revised 2013) — Guinea's pertambangan code establishing lingkungan obligations. EITI Guinea — Guinea has been EITI compliant, publishing reports on pertambangan revenues.

Note: Guinea's democratic institutions are suspended under military rule. The Conseil National de la Transition (CNT) serves as a transitional legislature. International channels: ECOWAS (Guinea suspended), African Union, international pertambangan company home-country oversight (UK, Canada, UAE, China). EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — will require EU companies to address human rights and lingkungan impacts in their supply chains.

GQ.s: Small petrostate ($12.8B PDB) with 21.57% combined oil and gas rents. One of Africa's highest per-capita PDBs but extreme inequality. Authoritarian rule (Obiang family since 1979 — world's longest-serving head of state). Oil production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Equatorial Guinea's oil production has declined ~75% from peak. As fields deplete, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between operators and government revenue melemah. Without new discoveries, the economic case for diversification becomes unavoidable.
bp: Economic diversification as oil depletes. Ketika production revenue falls below government operating costs, kepentingan yang tumpang tindih dalam continued extraction from depleting fields breaks — alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-field emisi disclosure through international operator reporting (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil). Making lingkungan costs visible makes W computable.
Tidak dapat dipatahkan by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.
a: Report through international operator compliance channels (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil)
Document lingkungan violations to international NGOs
Report corruption to US DOJ (several Obiang family members have faced kleptocracy investigations)
ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil: Apply global lingkungan standards to EG operations
Report Scope 1-3 emisi for EG operations in global ESG disclosures
Terapkan community development programs
Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons: Publish production and revenue data through EITI (EG was suspended from EITI in 2010)
Perkuat lingkungan standards for offshore operations
BEAC: Improve fiscal transparansi for hydrocarbon revenues
Support international anti-kleptocracy enforcement for misappropriated oil revenues
Advokasi melalui CEMAC for regional transparansi standards
Dorong untuk re-engagement with EITI transparansi requirements
Support international mechanisms for asset recovery and repatriation of stolen oil revenues
i: Note: Equatorial Guinea is one of the world's most otoriter states. No independent media, masyarakat sipil, or labor organizations exist. EG LNG (Equatorial Guinea LNG Holdings) — operates the LNG processing plant. International engagement: US Department of Justice Kleptocracy Asset Recovery Initiative has investigated Obiang family assets. Transparency International consistently ranks Equatorial Guinea among the most corrupt countries.
ExxonMobil — the largest oil operator in Equatorial Guinea. Marathon Oil — operates in offshore blocks. GEPetrol — the state national oil company.

Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas operations. Note: Equatorial Guinea was suspended from EITI in 2010 for insufficient progress on transparansi. BEAC (central bank of CEMAC zone) provides limited macroeconomic oversight.

Note: Equatorial Guinea under President Obiang (in power since 1979) has no democratic institutions. The Cámara de Representantes del Pueblo and Senate are rubber-stamp bodies. International channels: CEMAC, African Union, UN Human Rights Council. Several countries (US, France, Switzerland) have pursued legal action against Obiang family members for corruption and money laundering.

GR.s: EU ekonomi ($256.2B PDB) with dominant services sector (68% PDB, tourism-heavy). Low resource extraction. Post-debt-crisis recovery. Major shipping industry (Greek-owned fleet is the world's largest). Pertanian pada 3.8% PDB (olives, wine).

sectors.w: Greece's Mediterranean diet is being displaced by UPF consumption, contributing to rising obesity (Greece has one of Europe's highest childhood obesity rates). Ketika diet-related healthcare costs are published per product category through EOPYY health insurance data, W menjadi dapat dihitung.
bp: Terapkan front-of-pack health labelling to make welfare costs visible to consumers. Ketika UPF health costs are visible at point of sale, the kepentingan tumpang tindih between processors and retailers in high-margin UPF melemah.

EOPYY (National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) publication of diet-attributable disease costs per food category. Greece's national health system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Reformasiulation mandates can reduce ketergantungan sistem.

Document food safety violations
Use Greek pelapor law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937, 2022)
Greek food companies: Investasi di traditional Mediterranean diet products over UPF
Terapkan voluntary front-of-pack labelling
Commission health-impact assessments
Ministry of Health: Terapkan mandatory front-of-pack labelling
EFET: Perkuat enforcement of marketing claims
EOPYY: Publish diet-related health expenditure data
